question_text:
Let X be a discrete random variable with probability mass function $p \in \{ p _ { 0 } , p _ { 1 } \}$ 9
x78910
${ \pmb p } _ { 1 } ( { \pmb x } )$ 0.690.100.160.05
${ \pmb p } _ { \bf 0 } ( { \pmb x } )$ 0.900.050.040.01
To test $H _ { 0 } : p = p _ { 0 }$ against $H _ { 1 } : p = p _ { 1 }$ , the power of the most powerful test of size 0.05, based on $X$ , equals (round off to 2 decimal places).
solution:
To calculate the power of the most powerful test, we use the likelihood ratio test statistic and calculate the probability of rejecting $H _ { 0 }$ when $p = p _ { 1 }$ . The power is the probability that the test correctly rejects the null hypothesis. Based on the given values, the power is approximately:

 $ \mathrm { P o w e r = 0 . 2 0 . } $ 

Thus, the value is 0.20.
Quick Tip

To calculate the power of a hypothesis test, compute the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when the alternative hypothesis is true.
